question: What were the notable DeFi and RWA launches on Stellar in 2025?

golden.answer: A dated, illustrative 2025 launch set includes **WisdomTree CRDT** (2025-09-12), **Ondo USDY on Stellar** (announced/live 2025-09-17), **PayPal USD (PYUSD) on Stellar** (2025-09-18), and **Templar Protocol on Stellar** (2025-11-05). Cite a primary source for every row and label the row's state—announced, approved, technically integrated, listed, or publicly live—because those states are not interchangeable. Franklin Templeton announced a Singapore retail tokenized fund on Stellar in 2025, but the checked primary material did not establish one exact public go-live date. This is not an exhaustive census and the September grouping is not itself evidence of an 'inflection point.'
keyFacts: Provides a dated, explicitly illustrative 2025 set with one primary source per named row and separates announcement/approval/integration/listing from public availability.
avoid: Do NOT call the list exhaustive, promote a roadmap or approval into a live launch, invent an exact Franklin Singapore go-live date, or assert a September/Meridian inflection point as fact.
Do NOT misattribute issuer/product roles: USDY is an Ondo product, CRDT is WisdomTree's, and PYUSD is PayPal-branded and Paxos-issued.
golden.notes:
VERIFIED dated facts against dossier primary sources: WisdomTree CRDT (Sep 12 2025, Stellar+Ethereum, GLACI index), Ondo USDY (Sep 17 2025, 5.3% APY), PayPal PYUSD (Sep 18 2025) — clustered around Meridian 2025 in Rio (Sep 17-18). Also FT first Singapore retail tokenized fund (2025). Freshness:quarterly — list is time-bounded. 'Notable launches in period X' → lumenloop_search_content_semantic.
Also good if the answer: Notes the September 2025 cluster (USDY + PYUSD + CRDT around Meridian 2025 in Rio) as an inflection point for Stellar DeFi/RWA growth. | Attributes each launch to a dated source and flags the list is time-bounded.
Golden cites: A dated source for each named launch.
GT-19 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: retain the confirmed CRDT/USDY/PYUSD dates, add Templar as another verified live launch, make the roster illustrative, remove the unsupported inflection-point claim, and require row-level status/provenance. Franklin's exact public go-live date remains unpinned.

corroboration.claim: PYUSD launched on Stellar on 2025-09-18 as PayPal USD and is issued by Paxos; the named Paxos entity differs between the historical launch release and current product page.

evidence.ref: A: https://stellar.org/press/paypal-pyusd-is-now-available-on-stellar — 2025 launch date and Paxos Trust Company, LLC issuer attribution

evidence.ref: A: https://www.paxos.com/pyusd/ — current page says issued by Paxos Trust Company, N.A., while separately identifying some PayPal/Venmo services with the LLC

corroboration.claim: CRDT, USDY, PYUSD, and Templar have primary-source 2025 launch evidence, while the set is illustrative and distinct announcement/integration/listing states cannot be collapsed into live availability.

evidence.ref: A: issuer/operator launch releases from WisdomTree, Ondo, PayPal/SDF, and Templar

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The launch dates/products remain confirmed, but the inherited 'issuer' trap treated PYUSD=PayPal as legal issuance. PayPal is the product brand/sponsor and Paxos is the issuer. Entity-level naming is date-sensitive: SDF's 2025 launch release names Paxos Trust Company, LLC, while Paxos's current product page names Paxos Trust Company, N.A.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-19 primary 3254 and blind 3258; sibling sweep covered dated RWA/product launch cases.
rootCause: A small dated launch sample was promoted into a notable/exhaustive narrative and distinct deployment states were collapsed.
Eval-side role conflation: product brand/sponsor was labeled as legal issuer.
